title: Bump github.com/containernetworking/plugins from 0.8.1 to 0.8.2
body:
Bumps [github.com/containernetworking/plugins](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins) from 0.8.1 to 0.8.2.
Release notes *Sourced from [github.com/containernetworking/plugins's releases](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/releases).* > ## CNI Plugins v0.8.2 > This is a minor release of the CNI plugins. It includes some important bug fixes, as well as new features: > > ## New features: > - Support "args" in static and tuning ([#281](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/281)). > - Add Loopback DSR support, allow l2tunnel networks to be used with the l2bridge plugin ([#331](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/331)). > - host-local: return error if same ADD request is seen twice ([#328](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/328)). > - bandwidth: fix collisions ([#353](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/353)). > - Support ips capability in static and mac capability in tuning ([#343](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/343)). > - pkg/veth: Make host-side veth name configurable. ([#344](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/344)). > > ## Bug fixes: > - Fix: failed to set bridge addr: could not add IP address to \"cni0\": file exists ([#366](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/366)). > - host-device: revert name setting to make retries idempotent ([#357](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/357)). > - Vendor update go-iptables ([#358](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/358)). Vendor update go-iptables to obtain commit f1d0510cabcb710d5c5dd284096f81444b9d8d10 > Update go.mod & go.sub > - Remove link Down/Up in MAC address change to prevent route flush ([#364](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/364)). > - pkg/ip unit test: be agnostic of Linux version ([#349](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/349)). on Linux 4.4 the syscall error message is "invalid argument" not "file exists" > - bump containernetworking/cni to v0.7.1 ([#341](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/341)).
Commits - [`485be65`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/485be65581341430f9106a194a98f0f2412245fb) Merge pull request [#372](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/372) from squeed/fix-build - [`ca82120`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/ca821200196422b5d186fef7efe4e18b6c063956) win-bridge, win-overlay: remove extra import - [`c9e1c0c`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/c9e1c0c1edecfa407069a01059dab958ede6b709) Merge pull request [#281](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/281) from s1061123/dev/support-args - [`2d6d4b2`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/2d6d4b260a98973a206fde18bdde4f2511c72c60) Merge pull request [#366](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/366) from Random-Liu/fix-bridge-race - [`ad7c1d1`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/ad7c1d189bdbc071c85a86e2d511f012cd8104dd) Fix a race condition in the bridge plugin. - [`a069a5f`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/a069a5f1a3979c099d0f91efca24bd393077ecea) Support "args" in static and tuning - [`ccd683e`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/ccd683e1a3109adf19919291642a514f0dff0c6f) Merge pull request [#357](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/357) from mars1024/bugfix/host-device - [`a11cb62`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/a11cb626b0c136d900eec642bb0e59f4cc365e73) Merge pull request [#331](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/331) from nagiesek/LoopbackDsr - [`f36dbc2`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/f36dbc2031719c04c717e3cb0f2db69b7da20857) Merge pull request [#358](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/358) from mccv1r0/ipt-vendor - [`e9d511c`](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/commit/e9d511c5bcd7a1e312dceee82d9056a5ffd9d618) Merge pull request [#364](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/containernetworking/plugins/issues/364) from s1061123/fix/removeifdown - Additional commits viewable in [compare view](https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/compare/v0.8.1...v0.8.2)

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://api.dependabot.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=github.com/containernetworking/plugins&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=0.8.1&new-version=0.8.2)](https://dependabot.com/compatibility-score.html?dependency-name=github.com/containernetworking/plugins&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=0.8.1&new-version=0.8.2) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) --- **Note:** This repo was added to Dependabot recently, so you'll receive a maximum of 5 PRs for your first few update runs. Once an update run creates fewer than 5 PRs we'll remove that limit. You can always request more updates by clicking `Bump now` in your [Dependabot dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com).
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot use these labels` will set the current labels as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these reviewers` will set the current reviewers as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these assignees` will set the current assignees as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use this milestone` will set the current milestone as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot badge me` will comment on this PR with code to add a "Dependabot enabled" badge to your readme Additionally, you can set the following in your Dependabot [dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com): - Update frequency (including time of day and day of week) - Pull request limits (per update run and/or open at any time) - Out-of-range updates (receive only lockfile updates, if desired) - Security updates (receive only security updates, if desired) Finally, you can contact us by mentioning @dependabot.

comments.body:
Hi @dependabot-preview[bot]. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot-preview[bot]*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4087) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
